root_text.sn47.33:1.1: “Yesaṁ kesañci, bhikkhave, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā viraddhā, viraddho tesaṁ ariyo maggo sammā dukkhakkhayagāmī.
root_text.sn47.33:1.2: Yesaṁ kesañci, bhikkhave, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā āraddhā, āraddho tesaṁ ariyo maggo sammā dukkhakkhayagāmī.
root_text.sn47.33:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.33:2.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.33:2.6: Yesaṁ kesañci, bhikkhave, ime cattāro satipaṭṭhānā viraddhā, viraddho tesaṁ ariyo maggo sammā dukkhakkhayagāmī.
root_text.sn47.33:2.7: Yesaṁ kesañci, bhikkhave, ime cattāro satipaṭṭhānā āraddhā, āraddho tesaṁ ariyo maggo sammā dukkhakkhayagāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn47.33:1.1: “Mendicants, whoever has missed out on the four kinds of mindfulness meditation has missed out on the noble path to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn47.33:1.2: Whoever has undertaken the four kinds of mindfulness meditation has undertaken the noble path to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn47.33:2.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.33:2.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.33:2.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.33:2.6: Whoever has missed out on these four kinds of mindfulness meditation has missed out on the noble path to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn47.33:2.7: Whoever has undertaken these four kinds of mindfulness meditation has undertaken the noble path to the complete ending of suffering.”

variant_text.sn47.33:1.1: viraddho tesaṁ ariyo maggo → ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo (sya-all, mr)
